content.problemStatement:
You are given a weighted undirected graph. The vertices are enumerated from 1 to nnn. Your task is to find the shortest path between vertex 1 and vertex nnn.


Input Format

The first line contains two integers nnn and mmm (2 ≤ n ≤ 105, 0 ≤ m ≤ 105)(2 ≤ n ≤ 10^5, 0 ≤ m ≤ 10^5)(2 ≤ n ≤ 105, 0 ≤ m ≤ 105), where nnn is the number of vertices and mmm is the number of edges.
Following mmm lines contain one edge each in form ai,bia_i, b_iai​,bi​ and wiw_iwi​ (1≤ai,bi≤n,1≤wi≤106)(1 ≤ a_i, b_i ≤ n, 1 ≤ w_i ≤ 10^6)(1≤ai​,bi​≤n,1≤wi​≤106), where ai, bia_i,  b_iai​, bi​ are edge endpoints and wiw_iwi​ is the length of the edge.
It is possible that the graph has loops and multiple edges between pair of vertices.


Output Format
Write the only integer -1 in case of no path. Write the shortest path in opposite case. If there are many solutions, print any of them.
content.inputFormat: First line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each test case starts with a line containing two integers n and m (2 ≤ n ≤ 100000, 0 ≤ m ≤ 100000) – the number of vertices and edges. Then m lines follow, each containing three integers a, b and w (1 ≤ a, b ≤ n, 1 ≤ w ≤ 1000000) describing an undirected edge of weight w. The graph may contain loops and multiple edges.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print a single line. If there is no path from vertex 1 to vertex n, print -1. Otherwise print the vertices of any shortest path from 1 to n separated by a single space and ending with a space.

sampleTestCases.explanation: The shortest path from 1 to 5 goes through vertices 4 and 3 with total weight 1+3+1=5.
Path 1‑2‑3‑4 has total weight 1+2+1=4, which is minimal.
There is no edge connecting vertex 1 and vertex 2, so no path exists.

solution.approach: The graph is undirected with non‑negative edge weights, therefore Dijkstra's algorithm yields the shortest distances from the source vertex 1 to all other vertices. While relaxing edges we also store the predecessor of each vertex on the current best path. After the algorithm finishes, if the distance to vertex n is still infinite there is no path and we output -1. Otherwise we reconstruct the path by following predecessor links from n back to 1, reverse it and print it. Using a binary heap (Java PriorityQueue) gives O((n+m) log n) time per test case, which easily satisfies the limits. All data structures are cleared for each test case.
solution.timeComplexity: O((n + m) log n) per test case
solution.spaceComplexity: O(n + m) – adjacency list, distance array, predecessor array and priority queue

code.Java: import java.io.*;import java.util.*;public class Main{static class Edge{int to;int w;Edge(int to,int w){this.to=to;this.w=w;}}static class Node implements Comparable<Node>{int v;long d;Node(int v,long d){this.v=v;this.d=d;}public int compareTo(Node o){return Long.compare(this.d,o.d);}}public static void main(String[] args)throws Exception{BufferedReader br=new BufferedReader(new InputStreamReader(System.in));StringBuilder out=new StringBuilder();String line;while((line=br.readLine())!=null&&line.trim().isEmpty()){}if(line==null){System.out.print("");return;}int T=Integer.parseInt(line.trim());for(int tc=0;tc<T;tc++){while((line=br.readLine())!=null&&line.trim().isEmpty()){}if(line==null)break;StringTokenizer st=new StringTokenizer(line);int n=Integer.parseInt(st.nextToken());int m=Integer.parseInt(st.nextToken());List<Edge>[] g=new ArrayList[n+1];for(int i=1;i<=n;i++)g[i]=new ArrayList<>();for(int i=0;i<m;i++){while((line=br.readLine())!=null&&line.trim().isEmpty()){}StringTokenizer et=new StringTokenizer(line);int a=Integer.parseInt(et.nextToken());int b=Integer.parseInt(et.nextToken());int w=Integer.parseInt(et.nextToken());g[a].add(new Edge(b,w));g[b].add(new Edge(a,w));}long INF=Long.MAX_VALUE/4;long[] dist=new long[n+1];int[] prev=new int[n+1];Arrays.fill(dist,INF);Arrays.fill(prev,-1);PriorityQueue<Node> pq=new PriorityQueue<>();dist[1]=0;pq.add(new Node(1,0));while(!pq.isEmpty()){Node cur=pq.poll();if(cur.d!=dist[cur.v])continue;for(Edge e:g[cur.v]){long nd=cur.d+e.w;if(nd<dist[e.to]){dist[e.to]=nd;prev[e.to]=cur.v;pq.add(new Node(e.to,nd));}}}if(dist[n]==INF){out.append("-1\n");}else{int[] path=new int[n];int cnt=0;for(int v=n;v!=-1;v=prev[v]){path[cnt++]=v;}for(int i=cnt-1;i>=0;i--){out.append(path[i]).append(' ');}out.append('\n');}}System.out.print(out.toString());}}
